subject: English
skill: Story Elements, Theme, and Character Response

lesson.story_mission: Join the Story Rangers to map characters, settings, plot problems, solutions, traits, and themes.
lesson.objective: I can identify story elements, explain character responses, and find a theme using evidence.
lesson.concept_explanation: Readers understand literature by noticing who is in the story, where it happens, what problem appears, how characters respond, and what lesson the events teach. A theme is bigger than one detail because it tells what readers can learn from the whole story.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers may choose a small detail as the theme or confuse a feeling with a trait.
lesson.real_world_connection: Story elements help readers discuss books, recommend stories, and explain why characters make choices.
lesson.lesson_summary: Map the story, track the problem and solution, study character actions, and use evidence to name the theme.
lesson.mini_lesson: First find the characters and setting. Next sequence the problem and solution. Then connect actions to traits and use the ending to infer the theme.

audio.text: character, setting, plot, problem, solution, trait, theme
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Watch: Lena is the character, the windy hill is the setting, and the tangled string is the problem. The model shows how the solution and theme grow from the events.
worked_examples.correct_answer: Lena keeps trying and shares success.
worked_examples.passage: On Saturday, Lena carried a paper kite to the windy hill. The string tangled twice, but she took a slow breath and tried again. When the kite finally rose, Lena smiled and helped her little brother hold the string.
events: The kite string tangled
Lena tried again and helped her brother

audio.text: On Saturday, Lena carried a paper kite to the windy hill. The string tangled twice, but she took a slow breath and tried again. When the kite finally rose, Lena smiled and helped her little brother hold the string.
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Demo: Lena is patient because she takes a slow breath and tries again. That evidence supports the trait better than a feeling like happy.
action_evidence: took a slow breath and tried again

audio.text: Lena took a slow breath and tried again.
audio.label: Listen

hint_ladder: Reread the beginning for characters and setting.
Track the problem before the solution.
Use actions and the ending to infer traits and theme.

misconception_bank.character_trait_confusion: A reader names a feeling or one action instead of a stable character trait.
misconception_bank.problem_solution_confusion: A reader mixes up what went wrong with how the character fixed it.
misconception_bank.theme_detail_confusion: A reader chooses one detail instead of the bigger lesson.
misconception_bank.sequence_order_error: A reader retells events out of order.

story.text: Read this page to enter the Story Elements, Theme, and Character Response mission. Think about how careful readers notice the author’s clues before they answer. Press Start Mission when you are ready to read, listen, and build the skill step by step.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: Read this page to learn the strategy for characters, setting, plot, problem, solution, traits, and theme. Look at the model words and connect each vocabulary word to the reading job. Press Next to continue after you can explain the strategy in your own words.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Read this page while the worked example shows the prompt and model. Step one, read the text carefully. Step two, find the clue that answers the question. Step three, choose the answer and check why the final answer is supported. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: Read this page to try the demo with support. Use the visual model to organize the reading clues before answering. Press Next to continue when you can tell why the answer fits the text.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Read this page before practice. Listen to the passage when the Listen button helps, then press Read Question so the question is clear. Choose your answer when you are ready, and use the hint if you need help.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Read this page before the challenge. This question asks you to use the same strategy with less help. Press Read Question, reread the important part of the text, and answer carefully, then continue.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Read this page for the checkpoint. Show what you know by reading the text, pressing Read Question, and checking your evidence before you answer. Answer carefully, then continue to see your results.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Read this page to celebrate your progress. The badge means you practiced reading closely and explaining your thinking. Press Next to see your profile and think about where you can use this skill next.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: Read this page to review your learner profile. Look at the skill, the badge, and the next practice option. Continue to Skill Practice or go back to the hub when you are ready.
profile.label: Read This Page
